rows.resumely: 12 file-selected → 7 optimized → 1 preview-rendered → 3 exported (WP-50 contract, is_internal_tester excluded; n too small to test the 20% target, and the preview step is defective)
rows.runsmart: 3 saw the sign-in wall → 2 tapped → 1 in, 1 hard-failed 5x with Apple code 1000 on the live binary

rows.resumely: 145 reached → 20 tapped upload → 16 uploaded → 8 job → 2 optimized → 1 exported
rows.runsmart: 23 organic App Store users → 1 onboarded → 1 planned → 0 ran

rows.resumely: arrival → file selected: ~12-15 guests/wk arrive, 0-5 ever select a résumé. The cliff moved earlier than the old upload→optimization framing suggested
rows.runsmart: install → onboarding, and the cause is now named: the sign-in wall is not a persuasion step, it is a broken gate that returns Apple error 1000 to first-time users

rows.resumely: n/a — guests reach value before any account, which is why Resumely is measurable at all
rows.runsmart: Config CLEARED, failures REAL. All 5 chain links correct with artifacts; the App Store binary completed a native Apple sign-in through Supabase on 07-21 (status 200). But June converted 7/40 launchers (17.5%) vs July 0/~26 (p≈0.7%), outcomes partition perfectly across 13 devices, auth code unchanged builds 17-22, and every success is an already-authorized Apple ID. Cause is first-time authorization. Next: WP-52a instrument NSUnderlyingErrorKey + revoke-and-retry repro

rows.metric: Audit cross-check (2026-07-21) — why the PostHog AI number is 2.7x optimistic
rows.resumely: A PostHog-generated audit reported 12.8% activation (25/196). It excluded 4 email-matched persons; 54 of 199 persons (27%) carry is_internal_tester and generate 49% of all events. Clean equivalent is ~4.8% (7 optimization_completed / ~145 clean actives). The audit also invented its own activation definition, unaware the WP-50 contract lives in the repo rather than in PostHog
rows.runsmart: Same audit claimed sign_in_failed carries no diagnostics ('error and provider are null') and made fixing it a top-5 weekly priority. It queried property names that do not exist — the real ones (error_code=1000, error_domain, screen=sign_in_wall) are populated on all 12 events. Instrumentation is fine; the Apple config is not

rows.resumely: save_failed 21 events / 20 people, optimization_apply_failed 12/9, optimization_state_recovery_failed 24/12 — ALL with zero diagnostic properties (0 of 57 rows populated). Users who do activate then hit a blind wall
rows.runsmart: plan_generation ~29% failure rate (28 started → 12 succeeded, 8 failed). coach_message_sent silent for 30d while coach_thread_opened still fires — likely silent breakage

rows.resumely: 92% one-and-done (217/234 persons active in exactly one week over 60d); W2 return ~6%. WAU grew 6→63 over 7 weeks then fell to 34 — growth is almost entirely new users, not returning ones
rows.runsmart: D1 10.7% (9/84), D7/D30 not computable — 69 of 84 person IDs are anonymous installs that never authenticated, so the denominator is unusable

rows.resumely: Activation milestone optimized_preview_rendered fires for only 3 people in 60d while more people export than preview — funnel is non-monotonic, so the milestone is suspect
rows.runsmart: app_version/app_build now land correctly on the live build. Note: marketing_version is NOT in this project's taxonomy — plans referencing it should read $app_version / app_build

rows.resumely: 1.4.3 LIVE since 2026-07-19T21:47:02Z. 1.4.4 (14) SUBMITTED and UNDER ASC REVIEW (founder-confirmed 2026-07-21) — a UI/copy/Hebrew release carrying NO activation-measurement fix, so approval resets the exact-version cohort clock while the milestone is still broken

≥20 clean activations on a WORKING milestone — no deadline. Supersedes '20% by 08-01', which was untestable at n=12. 2026-08-01 is now a reporting checkpoint; 2026-09-01 triggers an activation-strategy revisit if nothing is measurable
rows.runsmart: ≥30% — but not measurable at all until the sign-in gate opens (WP-52 in progress)

activation.note: REFRESH 2026-07-21 (live PostHog, projects 171597 + 270848). Three things changed and they are not equally good news. (1) RunSmart's new instrumentation works and immediately caught the sign-in P0 reproducing on the LIVE 1.1.1 public binary — this closes the 'is it outage or persuasion' question in favour of outage, and it invalidates reading any RunSmart FTUX number as a product signal until sign-in is fixed. (2) Resumely's canonical WP-50 funnel is readable but n=12/30d and internally inconsistent (more exporters than preview-renderers), so the activation milestone optimized_preview_rendered is itself suspect and must be fixed before the 2026-08-01 EXD-015 target review means anything. (3) Store-verified release truth: RunSmart 1.1.1 live 2026-07-20T20:38:19Z, Resumely still 1.4.3 — though 1.4.4 events are already in PostHog, so a non-App-Store build is circulating. Prior clean-funnel row retained below for continuity. Clean-funnel row added 2026-07-19 from a founder/QA/bot-excluded PostHog read (projects 171597 + 270848, 90d) filed as executive-os/ACTIVATION-AUTOPSY-2026-07-19.md and per-repo docs/plans/2026-07-19-activation-cliff-fix-plan.md. Exclusions: founder person, +fable-qa*/+export-wall* aliases, is_internal_tester (45 persons / 1,216 events in Resumely), burst chains >=3 events/sec, and RunSmart behavioural founder/QA heuristics (>=6 sessions or >=3 app versions). Mature founder-excluded D7 from 2026-07-12 remains the decision contract; the clean-funnel row is diagnostic, not a replacement cohort. Correction: free_ats_completed DOES carry score_bucket — live 90d is 5x '0-40' + 5x '41-60', so every real free-ATS user saw <=60.

posthogDashboards.decisionSnapshot: P0 OUTAGE CONFIRMED 2026-07-21 on live 1.1.1: 5/5 Apple code-1000 failures at sign_in_wall on a real App Store install. Activation numbers downstream of the wall are not interpretable until this is fixed. Decision-grade mature D7 remains 0% (0/13 through 2026-07-12)
posthogDashboards.exclusions: Sign-in read is unfiltered by design — every code-1000 event matters regardless of who hit it; install-channel flags ($is_testflight/$is_sideloaded/$is_emulator) were checked per-event instead
posthogDashboards.cohortCutoff: Mature D7 through 2026-07-12 · live 1.1.1 sign-in read 2026-07-21
posthogDashboards.warning: The linked operational dashboard uses rolling 30-day charts, independent event totals and broader traffic. It can include founder signals and is not the mature ordered D7 cohort.

posthogDashboards.decisionSnapshot: Live WP-50 contract 2026-07-21: 12 file-selected → 7 optimized → 1 preview-rendered, 3 exported (30d, is_internal_tester excluded). Not decision-grade: n=12 cannot test the 20% target, and the funnel is non-monotonic. Decision-grade mature D7 remains 0% (0/73 through 2026-07-12)
posthogDashboards.exclusions: is_internal_tester persons excluded at the person level (any tagged event disqualifies the person for the window)
posthogDashboards.cohortCutoff: Mature D7 through 2026-07-12 · WP-50 contract refresh 2026-07-21
posthogDashboards.warning: Two retired events will silently produce false zeros: resume_uploaded lost its production call site in 1.4.3 (WP-46 Story 10), and resume_upload_succeeded sits behind the sign-in guard so it can never exceed sign_in_completed (WP-48 Defect B). The canonical denominator is resume_file_selected. Separately, optimized_preview_rendered — the activation milestone itself — appears to under-fire and is the next measurement fix.

top3.title: Verify RunSmart sign-in on the public build now
top3.body: Make one founder-device Sign in with Apple attempt and record the exact result. If it fails, inspect shipped provisioning, the App Store capability, and Supabase Apple provider configuration before changing app code.

top3.title: Finish the active Resumely lane, then continue the approved 1.5.0 sequence
top3.body: Do not duplicate Stories 7-8. After Story 8 integrates, verify the Story 9 evidence contract and deliver Stories 9-13 one at a time through WP-46. Stop before archive, TestFlight, or App Store actions.

top3.title: Protect the measurement contract while cohorts mature
top3.body: Merge the current evidence branches after review, record WP-31's actual publish timestamp and Israeli storefront baseline, and run the July 22/25 checkpoints without converting immature 0/0 cohorts into activation rates.

experiments.hypothesis: Rewriting the App Store description with approved Garmin sentence and tighter opening will produce a clear, credible listing that converts at ≥35% (product page → install) after first 100 impressions
Instrumenting onboarding_completed, plan_generated, and run_logged before launch will make the first 30 days of installs measurable, enabling data-driven iteration from week 2
Moving users from plan_generated to first run with a concrete first-run commitment and local reminder will improve first-run activation before D7 can be evaluated
The six WP-43 Tier A entry-funnel changes will increase the share of qualified landers who submit the free checker
Post-launch ASO cleanup, first-review prompt, subtitle/keyword review, and screenshot captions will improve App Store product page → install conversion once baseline impressions exist
Adding approved A-variant caption overlays to all 5 screenshot slots will improve App Store conversion rate vs bare UI screenshots, measured at next keyword report
Sending welcome + plan-nudge + 2-day no-show emails via Resend/Supabase will increase week-1 activation rate (plan generated → first run logged) vs no email baseline

Writing the Resumely App Store listing (subtitle Option A, keywords field v1, description v1) will produce a conversion-optimised listing ready for first submission and establish a baseline for subsequent A/B iteration
experiments.status: approved — ready to file in ASC

experiments.hypothesis: Producing a 5-slot screenshot brief with keyword-rich captions (indexed since 2025) and structured copy overlays will give the listing strong visual conversion signals from day one
experiments.status: approved — screenshots exported; PR #34 ready to merge; upload to ASC pending

experiments.hypothesis: Submitting Resumely to 5 AI/career directories (Futurepedia, TAAFT, Toolify, AI Tool Hunt, Launching Next) will produce initial backlinks and referral traffic to the web funnel that feeds App Store installs

Publishing a Hebrew subtitle, keyword field, promo text, and 5 screenshot captions (Fit-First framing, per WP-31) will improve Israeli storefront App Store product page → install conversion within a 21-day measurement window
experiments.status: published — measuring; founder-confirmed live 2026-07-15, actual publish timestamp and ASC baseline unlogged

models.source: July 2026 capability review (founder + ChatGPT draft). Pricing independently verified 2026-07-10: Claude via claude-api skill; GPT-5.6 / Grok 4.5 / Composer 2.5 via web search. All figures confirmed against the draft.
models.note: Model selection is a RECOMMENDATION, not a rule. The `harness` column shows where each model runs natively, but routes are advisory — you can run any task in whichever tool you choose (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, xAI) and pick a different model than the one suggested. Canonical policy: GLOBAL-TOOL-USAGE.md 'Model routing'.

harnesses.name: Claude Code
harnesses.detail: Native subagents (model: frontmatter), /model per session, workflows (opts.model per stage).

harnesses.name: Codex
harnesses.detail: GPT-5.6 family. Multi-step repo tasks, terminal/CI, cross-repo, prod verification, cross-vendor review of Claude work.

harnesses.name: Cursor Composer
harnesses.detail: Composer 2.5. Localized edits, boilerplate, mechanical refactors, UI iteration from an explicit plan.

harnesses.name: Grok (xAI)
harnesses.detail: Grok 4.5. Live web/X research, terminal-heavy work, parallel cheap exploration. Only if wired into the active harness.

models.role: Exceptional escalation — architecture authority, unresolved failures, final reasoning reviewer.
Architecture, ambiguous specs, hard debugging, difficult code review, large unfamiliar repos.
Everyday Claude agent — medium features, detailed specs, first-pass review.
Mechanical edits, status/progress sync, changelogs, classification.
Tool-heavy autonomous Codex tasks, terminal/CI failures, cross-repo, prod verification.
Fallback tier only — not a default route (Luna or Sol usually win on cost/success).
OpenAI high-volume default — summaries, docs, test gen, small fixes, first-pass impl.
Feature impl, terminal-heavy, CI/build, live web/X research, cheap parallel exploration.
Default cheap executor from an explicit plan — localized UI, boilerplate, cleanup.
Same intelligence as Standard at 6x cost — only when interactive latency blocks UI iteration.

matrix.task: Classification / summaries / status sync / mechanical edits
Small, clearly-scoped edit from an explicit plan
High-risk: auth / payments / DB / security / infra

models.crossVendorReview: For high-risk changes, implementer and reviewer must be different vendors: Composer/Grok/Luna → Opus 4.8 review; Claude → GPT-5.6 Sol review; GPT-5.6 Sol → Opus 4.8 or Fable 5 review.

usage.note: Claude Code: priced from on-disk usage (PRICES verified 2026-07-10 vs model-registry.json; Opus now $5/$25, was mis-priced at Claude-3-era $15/$75). Codex: activity only (no token counts persisted). Cursor: not captured (see README). Cache-read tokens (from long sessions) are typically the dominant cost driver.
